genre_refs: Gilbert Stuart 'Lansdowne' portrait of George Washington
description: A plain, full-frame reproduction of Gilbert Stuart's 'Lansdowne' portrait of George Washington (c. 1796). The classical full-length oil painting depicts Washington standing in black formal 18th-century attire with white ruffled shirt and cuffs, his right arm extended in an oratorical gesture, his left hand resting on a sword. He is surrounded by ornate Neoclassical furnishings: a gilded table draped in red velvet bearing an inkwell and quill, gilt-carved chair with an eagle/shield motif, a large red drapery, a stone column, and a stormy sky with a rainbow visible at upper right. There is no on-image text, no agency seal, and no added design elements or memetic overlay; it is simply the artwork posted as-is. Marked is_designed=false because it is a straight reproduction of an existing historical painting rather than an agency-designed graphic. Context (tweet text) commemorates the 1789 first U.S. presidential election and credits the painting as 'George Washington Lansdowne Portrait - Gilbert Stuart.'
